sourceMovie.title: The Machinist
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit The Machinist for the same reason it broke through in 2004: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Brad Anderson keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The thriller and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Fight Club
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Machinist clicked for you, Fight Club (1999) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Machinist?
faq.answer: Try Fight Club, The Fugitive, Candyman—each shares DNA with The Machinist while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Machinist?
faq.answer: The Machinist (2004) is best described as Thriller, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Machinist worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this thriller lane.
